hoist on scroll only, not on overflow (

)
breadcrumbs no longer hoist (translate/scale up into the header) just because their content was too long on small screens. The old
hoist
mode gated on
overflowed && scrollProgressed
, so a long crumb trail would shrink itself the moment it overflowed even without any scroll. Hoisting is now driven purely by scroll progress.
replace selection-dependent FABs with footer action bars (

When a user selects rows in a list, bulk actions used to hide inside the FAB menu. You'd select rows and not know an action became available. This PR moves those actions into a bar that fades in over the table footer next to the rows you just selected.
What you'll see
Sourcing Requests, Opportunities, Sourcing summary : bulk actions move into the footer bar; the FAB is gone (it had no other items).
Purchase Orders, RFQ list, RFQ Line, Contacts : FAB keeps only the Create action; bulk actions move into the footer bar.
Update RFQ Status now appears on the nested RFQ table where you actually select rows, not on the entity FAB.
Sourcing Requests list — All / Offer Request / Purchase Request / Offers tabs

What changed
Multiline

FormikText
paste handling no longer treats every email as "tabular content". When the clipboard contains an email body (prose around a small inline table, or a body wrapped in a layout table — both common in Outlook/Gmail HTML), the paste now falls through to the browser's normal paste, preserving the full text exactly as it appeared in the email.
What still works
Pasting genuine tables — from Excel, Google Sheets, web tables, etc. — continues to be auto-formatted into aligned columns inside the textarea, same as before.
How to verify
Open any email in Outlook / Gmail that contains a greeting, a short body, and at least one row/table-like item, plus a signature.
Select the entire message and copy.
In CRM, open

Create Activity
(or any modal with a multiline Notes field) and paste into Notes.
the full message text lands in the field — greeting, body, item line, and signature.
copy a few cells from Excel/Sheets and paste into the same field — should still format as an aligned table.
